item.text:
Fixed `RateLimiter.allow()` in [demo/rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-clean-test/demo/rate_limiter.py:14) so expired timestamps are pruned from `self.calls` before checking the limit.

Added a regression test in [tests/test_rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-clean-test/tests/test_rate_limiter.py:1) covering rejection inside the window and cleanup after expiration.

Verified:
`python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` passes.
`python3 -m compileall demo harness kt tests` passes.

Note: the repo had tracked `harness/__pycache__` files dirty, and `compileall` touched more of them. I removed untracked bytecode dirs, but did not overwrite tracked dirty files.

--- Learning check ---

What invariant should `RateLimiter.calls` maintain after every `allow()` call, and why does that prevent the original memory growth bug?
